channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-13
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. Notably, the `statcheck` and `docpluck` projects have made strides in updating their features and fixing issues, with `statcheck` incorporating changes from its remote repository and `docpluck` recovering residual prose shapes and addressing affiliation remnants. Meanwhile, `coarse` has re-enabled Fable 5 in its model picker, and `regcheck` has modularized its precheck component into a separate application. Additionally, `escicheck` has synchronized with the `effectcheck` version 0.6.13, and smaller updates have been made to `auto-zcurve` and `output-it-forward`, including revisions to validation notes and the addition of a Chrome Webstore link. These connections and updates demonstrate the collaborative spirit and ongoing development within our community.

21 commits across 2 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: statcheck

Most active:

regcheck20 commits
metacheck1 commits

item.title: statcheck: activity update
item.description:
MicheleNuijten/statcheck

The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was worked on, where a merge was performed from the same branch of the remote repository. This merge was done to incorporate changes from the remote `feature-non-apa-newer` branch into the local branch. Additionally, on the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, "admin updates" were made, as specified in the commit message. The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch is the only branch that was modified in these commits. The changes made in these commits are limited to the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, with no other branches affected.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The `staging` branch was worked on, where a commit was made to "separate precheck as its own app", indicating a significant architectural change to the repository. The precheck functionality was isolated into a separate application, as per the commit message. No other branches were modified in this set of commits. This change suggests a modularization effort, focusing on the precheck component. The commit does not mention any other branches or components being affected by this change.

1 commit(s) today:

  • cceb94d [staging] separate precheck as its own app — Jamie Cummins
  • a430ad4 [staging] split precheck and regcheck domains — Jamie Cummins
  • 4e45f4f [staging] add precheck functionality — Jamie Cummins
  • ad6c3aa [staging] remove dimension numbers for defaults — Jamie Cummins
  • c0b987d [staging] change RR flow — Jamie Cummins
  • ffa8ea4 [staging] strip beta references — Jamie Cummins
  • 2751f19 [staging] phase 5 prod prep — Jamie Cummins
  • 5092647 [staging] minor additions preclin — Jamie Cummins
  • 820b166 [staging] phase 3 prod prep — Jamie Cummins
  • 575f010 [staging] commit phase 2 prod push — Jamie Cummins
  • cfeac5d [staging] roll back RR overkill — Jamie Cummins
  • 35075aa [staging] third phase of RR creation — Jamie Cummins
  • 6d30479 [staging] stage 2 RR dev — Jamie Cummins
  • 60f395c [staging] first phase: registered report mode — Jamie Cummins
  • 2363d45 [staging] small fix — Jamie Cummins
  • 8277b4d [staging] budget-saving changes — Jamie Cummins
  • f8a0f65 [staging] improve specificity on 'missing' judgements — Jamie Cummins
  • f02456c [staging] update default openai model — Jamie Cummins
  • 90c62a8 [staging] aesthetic bug fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • efb74a8 [staging] improve backend handling of insufficient evidence — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →
